mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of instances where the unemployment rate exceeds the 6.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which translates into positive equity returns over a quarter-ahead horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in retail sales and housing starts yield negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects improving factory utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term market returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a win rate above 52 percent in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation rises while the federal funds rate remains near zero, negative real interest rates emerge, boosting equity relative to fixed income and driving positive forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Negative real rate environments produce negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth series shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP signals an economic downturn that triggers recession risk premiums and earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP contraction periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 58 percent of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly expansions in industrial output and retail sales confirm broad-based economic momentum, reducing uncertainty and generating positive short-term market returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Co-movement of industrial output and retail sales yields a negative directional accuracy below 48 percent in out-of-sample testing.
